Title: Lysine biofortification in rice by modulating feedback inhibition of aspartate kinase and dihydrodipicolinate synthase

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RNAi inhibition/down-regulation of LKR/SDH reduces lysine catabolism and, when combined with mutant AK2 and DHDPS1 expression, greatly increases free lysine accumulation in mature seeds.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bifunctional lysine ketoglutarate reductase/saccharopine dehydrogenase catabolic enzyme
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi suppression in transgenic rice; qRT-PCR; Western blot; HPLC amino acid profiling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: analyzed in 15 DAF developing seeds; strongly down-regulated in 35ADL and 35A2D1L lines
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LKR/SDH is the lysine catabolic target suppressed in polygenic constructs. 2. Its transcript and protein levels were dramatically reduced in developing seeds of transgenic rice. 3. Suppression of LKR/SDH together with expression of feedback-insensitive AK2 and DHDPS1 enabled ~58-fold free lysine increase in mature seeds.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: essential amino acid for human and livestock nutrition
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious negative effects on major agronomic traits, seed appearance, or yield were observed in the tested stacked lines.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a catabolic suppression component to stack with feedback-insensitive lysine biosynthetic genes for high-lysine rice improvement.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied as part of the 35ADL and 35A2D1L stacked strategy rather than as a standalone focal gene in the Results.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: aspartate kinase; lysine-sensitive allosteric enzyme with modified lysine-binding domain
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: lysine biosynthetic branch of the aspartate family pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key rate-limiting enzyme in lysine biosynthesis and a major feedback-regulated control point
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: phosphorylates aspartate in the initial step of the aspartate-family amino acid pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor flux toward lysine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Feedback-insensitive AK2 expression promotes free lysine accumulation in rice seeds; strongest single AK mutant effect among AK constructs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: site-directed mutagenesis; recombinant expression in E. coli with AEC resistance assay; Agrobacterium-mediated rice transformation; Northern/Western blot; HPLC amino acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Gt1 promoter (endosperm-specific) in monogenic lines; CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) in 35A2D1L stacked lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highly expressed in developing seeds at 15 DAF in transgenic rice
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: AEC treatment (12 mM) in E. coli functional assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AK2 carries a Ser449Leu substitution in the predicted lysine-binding domain. 2. Recombinant AK2 conferred resistance to 12 mM AEC in E. coli, indicating reduced lysine feedback inhibition. 3. Endosperm expression of AK2 increased free lysine up to 6.6-fold in mature rice seeds. 4. Stacking AK2 with DHDPS1 and LKR/SDH RNAi generated ~58-fold free lysine increase without obvious agronomic penalty.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: essential amino acid for human and animal nutrition
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No statistically significant penalties in 1000-grain weight, seed setting rate, grains per panicle, tiller number, plot yield, or seed appearance in stacked lines.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising endogenous edited allele for rice and cereal biofortification; suitable for stacking with DHDPS1 and LKR/SDH suppression or precise base editing.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AK2 was more effective than AK1 in raising seed free lysine; mutation is endogenous rather than heterologous.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: dihydrodipicolinate synthase; lysine-sensitive allosteric enzyme with modified lysine-binding domain
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key rate-limiting/committed-step enzyme in lysine biosynthesis and highly feedback sensitive to lysine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses aspartate-semialdehyde with pyruvate to form dihydrodipicolinate in the lysine-specific branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms committed precursor for lysine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Feedback-insensitive DHDPS1 strongly promotes free lysine accumulation in rice seeds and is the most effective single DHDPS mutant tested.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: site-directed mutagenesis; recombinant expression in E. coli with AEC resistance assay; Agrobacterium-mediated rice transformation; Northern/Western blot; HPLC amino acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Gt1 promoter (endosperm-specific) in monogenic lines; CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) in 35A2D1L stacked lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highly expressed in developing seeds at 15 DAF in transgenic rice
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: AEC treatment (12 mM) in E. coli functional assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DHDPS1 carries a Ser124Asn substitution in the predicted lysine-binding domain. 2. Recombinant DHDPS1 conferred resistance to 12 mM AEC in E. coli, indicating reduced lysine feedback inhibition. 3. Endosperm expression of DHDPS1 increased free lysine up to 21.7-fold in mature seeds. 4. Stacking DHDPS1 with AK2 and LKR/SDH RNAi produced the highest lysine accumulation (~58-fold over WT).
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: essential amino acid for human and animal nutrition
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious agronomic or seed-quality penalty observed in stacked lines.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value endogenous target for precision editing or transgenic stacking to produce high-lysine cereals.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among five DHDPS mutants, DHDPS1 gave the strongest lysine increase.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: phosphorylates aspartate in the initial pathway step
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of native rice AK modestly increases free lysine in mature seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic overexpression; Northern/Western blot; HPLC amino acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Gt1 promoter (endosperm-specific); CaMV 35S promoter in 35ADL stacked construct
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highly expressed in 15 DAF developing seeds of transgenic rice
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Native AK overexpression increased free lysine 1.6- to 1.9-fold in mature seeds. 2. Native AK contributed to lysine enhancement in the 35ADL stack, though less effectively than mutant AK2. 3. Feedback-insensitive modification improved efficacy beyond native overexpression.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core experimentally tested enzyme comparator.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses aspartate-semialdehyde and pyruvate to form dihydrodipicolinate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms committed precursor for lysine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of native rice DHDPS increases free lysine in mature seeds, but less strongly than the best feedback-insensitive mutant.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic overexpression; Northern/Western blot; HPLC amino acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Gt1 promoter (endosperm-specific); CaMV 35S promoter in 35ADL stacked construct
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highly expressed in 15 DAF developing seeds of transgenic rice
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Native DHDPS overexpression increased free lysine by 2.0- to 4.4-fold in mature seeds. 2. Native DHDPS contributed to increased lysine in the 35ADL stack. 3. Feedback-insensitive DHDPS1 was substantially more effective than native DHDPS.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core experimentally tested enzyme comparator.
